title: Ogre
content:

{{Quote|You're not a friend of that Ogre, are you?|A Zandor Rocker to Wicket and Teebo|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
'''Ogres''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of large [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with stocky, well-muscled physiques. One specimen, named [[Gantu]], had pale, warty skin, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]], and unkempt black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] on his small head. That Ogre inhabited the land of [[Zandor]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], where he raided other sentient beings from his base in a large cave. The Ogre cut down the region's [[sunnydew flower]]s, which cast Zandor into permanent drought and angered the resident [[Zandor Rocker]]s.

Gantu's raids were cut short when the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], stole the [[bridge crystal]] that Gantu used to create the [[Rainbow Bridge]] that allowed him to cross the [[Gorge of Fire]] and enter Ewok territory. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] was destroyed for good in a fight with the Ewoks [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]], cutting the Ogre off from Ewok lands indefinitely.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Gantu.png|thumb|left|200px|Ogre teeth seemed to grow from their lips.]]
Ogres were large, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who stood roughly 3.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. At least one member of the [[Species/Legends|species]], [[Gantu]], had pale, greenish skin covered in warts. Ogre bodies were stocky but well muscled. Their [[Limb/Legends|arms]] were strong enough to carry large items, and their legs were long enough to walk longer distances than smaller species, such as [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], could. The head was small in comparison to the rest of the body, an effect that was magnified by the thick neck it sat upon. Ogres had four fingers per hand. The Ogre Gantu wore simple clothing: a green [[shirt]], an orange [[Belt/Legends|belt]], red pants, and orange shoes.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

The Ogre face featured pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]], close-set [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a small [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and a wide [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with a red tongue. Their few [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] appeared to grow from their lips rather than their gums. The Ogre Gantu had black eyebrows and a few scraggly black hairs growing from the tips of his ears and the top of his head.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Me afraid of dark!|Gantu|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
[[File:Gantu_cave.png|thumb|right|270px|Gantu the Ogre lived in a large cave.]]
At least one Ogre lived in the [[Zandor]] region of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. He inhabited a large cave with a tall ceiling so that he could comfortably stand at his full height. The Ogre made a living by raiding settlements of other sentients for provisions.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

Whether through robbery or manufacture, Endor's Ogre had access to simple items, including sleeping furs, storage pots, and stone bowls. He wielded a spiked wooden [[Club/Legends|club]] in combat. Ogres were capable of learning alien [[Language/Legends|languages]]. The Ogre Gantu, for example, spoke [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]], albeit with broken fluency.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

Ogres ate various foodstuffs, including [[Honey/Legends|honey]]. Nevertheless, they had no compunctions about eating other sentient beings, as Gantu demonstrated by chasing down Ewoks for his dinner bowl. The beings had a tendency to drool, and at least one specimen suffered from nyctophobia, a strong fear of night and the darkness associated with it.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

==History==
{{Quote|Long ago we were plagued by the evil Ogre, Gantu, who lived across the Gorge of Fire in the barren land of Zandor! Gantu would steal our supplies of honey… and when that was gone his appetite would turn to the taste of Ewok people!|Logray|<ref name="Ewoks 1" />}}
[[File:Chirpa_robs_Gantu.png|thumb|left|270px|Chief Chirpa stole the bridge crystal from Gantu and prevented the Ogre from crossing the Rainbow Bridge.]]
The Ogre Gantu settled in the land of Zandor on the Forest Moon of Endor. His fear of darkness led him to cut down the region's [[sunnydew flower]]s, the [[Glowing nectar|nectar]] of which he used to light up his cave. The act also dried out the region, killed off most of its plant life, and earned Gantu the enmity of a group of sentients known as the [[Zandor Rocker]]s.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

At some point, Gantu gained possession of the [[bridge crystal]], a prismatic stone that, when held to the sun, created a [[Rainbow Bridge]] and allowed passage across the [[Gorge of Fire]] to Ewok lands. Gantu began raiding the settlement [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], where he stole honey and Ewoks to eat. One night, Gantu awoke to find the Ewok [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], in his cave. Chirpa fled Zandor, taking with him the bridge crystal and some sunnydew nectar. With no access to the Rainbow Bridge, Gantu was confined to his home territory. He swore revenge on the Ewoks.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

Years later, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which include the appearance of the Ogre [[Gantu]] in [[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' 1]], to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Gantu once again found thieves in his cave: the Ewoks [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] and a single Zandor Rocker. In the mêlée that ensued, the bridge crystal was destroyed, but Gantu became doused in sunnydew nectar. His body glowed, so he happily ran off with no fear of dark places. The permanent destruction of the bridge crystal brought his raids into Ewok territory to an end.<ref name="Ewoks 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ogre Gantu appears in [[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic book from [[Marvel Comics]].<ref name="Ewoks 1" /> The story was written by [[David Manak]], illustrated by [[Warren Kremer]], and first published on [[January 29]], [[1985]].<ref name="Marvel Age">{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=Vol. 1 No. 25|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[April]] [[1985]]}}</ref>
